definition: Permanent, opaque, namespaced identifier for the element. Never reused, never re-pointed at a different concept.
rationale: Variable names get renamed and labels get reworded. Without a stable identifier there is no way to prove that a value collected in 2026 and a value collected in 2029 mean the same thing.

definition: The name the element carries in the collection platform.
rationale: What analysts and exporters actually type. Mutable by design; element_id is the anchor.

definition: Short display label shown to the person answering or reviewing.
rationale: Distinguished from definition so that wording can be improved without implying a change of meaning.

definition: A full definition of the concept that stands on its own, without reference to the label or the form.
example: Whether the malignancy was confined to its site of origin, extended to adjacent structures, or had spread to distant sites, as assessed at the time of initial diagnosis.
rationale: This is the column that prevents two sites collecting different things under one name. It is the single most frequently omitted column and the most expensive omission.

definition: The form or module the element belongs to.
rationale: Groups elements for consent, review, and export scoping.

definition: Semantic version of this element definition.
rationale: A change to permissible values is a breaking change for anyone analyzing across the boundary. Version it explicitly.

definition: active, deprecated, or superseded.
rationale: Deprecated elements stay in the dictionary. Deleting them destroys the ability to interpret historical data.

definition: element_id of the predecessor or successor.
rationale: Makes a chain of definition changes traceable rather than inferred.

definition: ISO dates.
rationale: Pairs with element_version for audit.

definition: Named role accountable for the definition and for approving changes.
rationale: An unowned dictionary drifts. A named steward is what makes the review cycle real.

definition: string, integer, decimal, date, boolean, coded.
rationale: Basic validation and load typing.

definition: Widget: radio, checkbox, dropdown, text, notes, calc, slider.
rationale: Determines whether an element is single or multi-valued, which changes the export shape.

definition: Unit of measure with its UCUM code where applicable.
rationale: Dose fields without units are a recurring source of silent error.

definition: Named, versioned value set rather than an inline list.
rationale: Named value sets can be reused and reviewed. Inline lists get copied and diverge.

definition: Code and display pairs.
rationale: Stored as code plus display so that relabeling does not change stored data.

definition: Whether the element is required, and the condition under which it is shown.
rationale: Missingness cannot be interpreted without knowing whether the element was ever asked.

definition: The terminology the element binds to.
rationale: Binding is what makes the element interoperable rather than merely documented.

definition: The specific concept the element or value represents.
rationale: Enables automated mapping into a common data model without human interpretation at load time.

definition: NCI caDSR Common Data Element public identifier.
rationale: Aligns this registry with NCI convention and with the caDSR reference already used elsewhere in this project.

definition: NAACCR Volume II item number where an equivalent exists.
rationale: Lets registry data be compared with central cancer registry data without a bespoke mapping exercise.

definition: participant survey, FHIR, C-CDA, narrative extraction, computed, registry constant, or external linkage.
rationale: The column that makes provenance analyzable rather than anecdotal. Required for every element that leaves the registry.

definition: Resource and element the value is read from.
rationale: Turns the mapping from prose into something a pipeline can be tested against.

definition: Plain-language statement of how the value is computed.
example: Stage assigned metastatic where any Condition carries ICD-10-CM C77-C79.
rationale: Anyone reviewing an export must be able to reconstruct how a value was reached without reading source code.

definition: Module and function implementing the rule.
rationale: Ties documentation to running code so the two can be checked against each other.

definition: auto, derive-then-confirm, survey, or human step.
rationale: Drives the attestation interface: it determines what a participant is shown for confirmation.

definition: Whether a human must confirm the value before it leaves the registry.
rationale: The operational expression of the attestation model.

definition: Whether provenance is stored per value instance, not only per element.
rationale: A single element may be FHIR-derived for one participant and survey-answered for another. Element-level provenance alone cannot represent that.

definition: OMOP CDM domain.
rationale: Places the element in the common data model that the analytic layer queries.

definition: Target table and column.
rationale: Makes the ETL specification reviewable independently of the code.

definition: Standard concept identifier.
rationale: Standard concepts are what network studies join on.

definition: Whether the original source code and text are preserved alongside the mapped concept.
rationale: Mapping decisions get revisited. Discarding the source value makes remapping impossible.

definition: internal only, CURE ID submission, aggregate public, or participant-facing.
rationale: Consent scoping and export filtering both read this column. It is what makes a disclosure decision auditable.

definition: Transformation applied before release: age band, year only, interval from index, suppress below threshold, free-text review, or none.
rationale: Privacy handled as a property of the element rather than as a step someone remembers to apply.

definition: Minimum cell size below which the value is suppressed.
rationale: Rare cancers with rare primary sites make small cells identifying.

definition: Date the element last received a re-identification review.
rationale: Public repositories change. A review that never repeats is a review that expires quietly.

definition: Target variable name and CRF step in the CURE ID instrument.
rationale: The join key between the internal dictionary and the submission dictionary.

definition: How each internal permissible value collapses into a CURE ID response option.
rationale: Value-level mapping is where most export defects originate. Documenting it makes them testable.

definition: Whether the mapping loses information, and what is lost.
example: yes: TNM detail collapsed to three categories
rationale: Records what the receiving repository will not have, so that no one later mistakes the CRF value for the full clinical picture.

definition: Target or observed completeness, with the denominator stated.
example: ~40% of participants with a connected record
rationale: Sets expectations before analysis and surfaces pipeline regressions.

definition: Constraint applied at entry or load.
rationale: Cheap to specify, expensive to omit.

definition: Documented caveats a downstream user needs in order to interpret the element correctly.
example: Administrative gender substituted where the birth sex extension is absent.
rationale: The column that carries honest caveats forward to people who were not in the room when the decision was made.
